posts.hook: 28%. That's the share of ransomware victims who paid ransoms in 2025 — down from 63% in 2024. The biggest year-over-year drop in payment rates we've seen, yet 2025 marked the most active attack
posts.body:
28%. That's the share of ransomware victims who paid ransoms in 2025 — down from 63% in 2024. The biggest year-over-year drop in payment rates we've seen, yet 2025 marked the most active attack year on record.

Over 8,000 organizations were publicly named on leak sites. Attacks rose 50% year-over-year according to Chainalysis data. Total on-chain payments fell to $820 million.

But here's what's unsettling: fewer victims paying doesn't mean less risk. It means threat actors are adapting their monetization strategies. The exfiltrated data still has value — they're just finding new ways to extract it.

#ThreatIntelligence #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #CISO
posts.cta: Add link to full threat intelligence report in first comment

visual.headline: Ransomware Payment Rates: The Historic Collapse

posts.hook: $1,427 to $439. That's how far initial access prices have fallen from Q1 2023 to Q1 2026 — a 69% deflation driven by automation, AI tooling, and oversupply from infostealer logs.
posts.body:
$1,427 to $439. That's how far initial access prices have fallen from Q1 2023 to Q1 2026 — a 69% deflation driven by automation, AI tooling, and oversupply from infostealer logs.

DWIQ operational data shows the average network access price collapsed as the market flooded with compromised credentials. But premium access — domain admin, Citrix environments, cloud consoles — still commands thousands to tens of thousands.

The economics are clear: when access becomes commodity-priced, threat actors pivot to volume operations and alternative monetization strategies.

#ThreatIntelligence #AccessBrokers #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb
posts.cta: Share insights on how access price trends affect your security posture

posts.hook: $75 million. The largest known ransom payment in recent history went to Dark Angels group, while median payments rose 368% year-over-year. Fewer victims are paying, but those
posts.body:
$75 million. The largest known ransom payment in recent history went to Dark Angels group, while median payments rose 368% year-over-year. Fewer victims are paying, but those who do are paying substantially more.

This creates a dangerous dynamic: organizations that refuse to pay aren't eliminating their risk — they're shifting it. Threat actors are developing sophisticated platforms to monetize exfiltrated data through structured resale rather than one-time extortion.

The binary choice of "pay or don't pay" no longer captures the full threat landscape.

#Ransomware #CISO #ThreatIntelligence #RiskManagement

visual.headline: 2025: Most Active Ransomware Year Despite Payment Rate Collapse

posts.hook: October 2025: DarkWebIQ operatives first learned about a new criminal marketplace from SnowTeam, an initial access broker with ties to Akira ransomware. The platform wasn't
posts.body:
October 2025: DarkWebIQ operatives first learned about a new criminal marketplace from SnowTeam, an initial access broker with ties to Akira ransomware. The platform wasn't designed for traditional name-and-shame tactics.

It was built to monetize exfiltrated data.

March 2026: SnowTeam advertised Leak Bazaar publicly on Exploit forum. The post was deleted. SnowTeam was banned for advertising ransomware products. Similar removals occurred on ReHub.

But infrastructure adapts. SnowTeam migrated to T1erOne forum, where Qilin, Medusa, and other major RaaS groups now advertise. A 10% referral program launched. Any ransomware group can now supply data.

What started as an Akira-specific tool evolved into ecosystem infrastructure. The economics drove the evolution.

#ThreatIntelligence #DarkWeb #Ransomware #CyberCrime
posts.cta: Track how criminal infrastructure evolves when traditional forums restrict access

visual.headline: Leak Bazaar Evolution: From Single Group Tool to Ecosystem Infrastructure

data.subtitle: How SnowTeam's data monetization platform adapted to market demands and forum restrictions

posts.hook: When Exploit forum banned SnowTeam for advertising Leak Bazaar, something predictable happened. The infrastructure didn't disappear — it migrated. T1erOne became the new
posts.body:
When Exploit forum banned SnowTeam for advertising Leak Bazaar, something predictable happened. The infrastructure didn't disappear — it migrated. T1erOne became the new home.

DWIQ operatives observed Qilin, Medusa, Hyflock, Gunra, and "The Gentlemen's RaaS" all advertising on T1erOne. The forum maintains a public blacklist of banned affiliates — two Nova affiliates and one Qilin affiliate at last count.

One detail stood out: a blacklisted Nova affiliate was identified as a researcher.

This pattern repeats constantly in the ecosystem. Top-tier forums enforce restrictions, infrastructure relocates to emerging platforms. The migration doesn't reduce access offers — it redistributes them across different venues while maintaining operational continuity.

#ThreatIntelligence #CyberCrime #DarkWeb #SecurityOperations
posts.cta: Monitor how threat infrastructure adapts when primary communication channels face restrictions

posts.hook: The numbers tell one story. The individual cases tell another. Jaguar Land Rover: $2.5 billion in estimated losses. Scattered Spider's Marks & Spencer breach:
posts.body:
The numbers tell one story. The individual cases tell another. Jaguar Land Rover: $2.5 billion in estimated losses. Scattered Spider's Marks & Spencer breach: prolonged operational disruption and significant stock depreciation. Interlock's DaVita attack: 2.7 million patient records exposed.

These weren't just statistics in the 28% of victims who refused to pay. They were organizations that gambled on containment and lost to secondary monetization.

The Dark Angels group's $75 million payday proves the other side: when critical infrastructure or mission-essential operations are at stake, the calculus changes completely.

Both outcomes — devastating losses from refusal and record payments for compliance — illustrate why the binary "pay or don't pay" framework no longer captures the real decision tree.

#Ransomware #IncidentResponse #CISO #RiskManagement
posts.cta: Evaluate how your incident response plans address both payment and non-payment scenarios

posts.hook: Late March 2026: DarkWebIQ operatives were offered a $50,000 distributed brute-force system through direct engagement with SnowTeam. The technical specifications read like
posts.body:
Late March 2026: DarkWebIQ operatives were offered a $50,000 distributed brute-force system through direct engagement with SnowTeam. The technical specifications read like enterprise software documentation.

Integrated Masscan for network scanning. Automated honeypot filtering. Proxy rotation with intelligent load management. Python-based result dumping with Kerberos extraction. Web-based control panel for monitoring CPU load and managing discovered access.

Eight enterprise protocols in one package: Fortinet, SonicWall, Cisco, WatchGuard, GlobalProtect, Sophos, RDWeb, and Citrix. Source code included. Lifetime technical support provided. Custom development available.

This isn't underground tooling anymore. It's productized software with enterprise pricing, professional support, and commercial development practices. The IAB market has industrialized.

#ThreatIntelligence #AccessBrokers #VPNSecurity #InfoSec

posts.body:

visual.headline: Criminal Tooling Goes Enterprise: Industrial-Scale Automation

posts.hook: Leak Bazaar isn't just another data dump site. It's a four-stage processing pipeline designed to convert raw exfiltrated data into structured, sellable intelligence products.
posts.body:
Leak Bazaar isn't just another data dump site. It's a four-stage processing pipeline designed to convert raw exfiltrated data into structured, sellable intelligence products.

→ STAGE 1: AUTOMATED FILTERING
ML-assisted removal of system debris (OS backups, DLLs, ISOs) combined with NLP-based text analysis of document arrays. The goal: eliminate noise, surface value.

→ STAGE 2: DBMS REVERSE ENGINEERING
Server-side parsing of raw database dumps from SQL, SAP, Oracle exports into structured Excel/CSV outputs. Financial transactions, payroll records, counterparty data — all formatted for buyers.

→ STAGE 3: CATEGORIZATION
Processed data segmented into buyer-oriented categories: quarterly financial reports, M&A data, R&D material, source code, personal data. No more browsing through raw dumps.

→ STAGE 4: MANUAL VALIDATION
In-house analyst review before listing. Quality control that generates structured analytical reports identifying OFAC violations, shadow accounting, unpublished financial results.

Two purchasing models: exclusive (full price, data removed after sale) or shared (lower price, data remains available for resale). Revenue split: 70% to supplier, 30% to platform.

This represents industrialization of data monetization. Organizations refusing ransoms can no longer assume their data becomes worthless.

#ThreatIntelligence #DataBreach #Ransomware #CISO #SecurityOperations
posts.cta: Consider how your incident response plans address structured data processing and resale

posts.hook: Three data points tell the story of ransomware's economic evolution in 2025: 28% payment rate. 8,000+ victims named on leak sites. $75 million single ransom payment.
posts.body:
Three data points tell the story of ransomware's economic evolution in 2025: 28% payment rate. 8,000+ victims named on leak sites. $75 million single ransom payment.

→ THE VOLUME PARADOX
Attacks increased 50% year-over-year despite historic low payment rates. Total on-chain payments dropped to $820 million (Chainalysis). More attacks, fewer successful extortions, yet the ecosystem expanded.

→ THE PAYMENT BIFURCATION
While overall payment rates collapsed from 63% to 28%, median ransom payments rose 368% year-over-year. The Dark Angels group's $75 million payday illustrates the new dynamic: fewer victims pay, but those who do often pay substantially more.

→ THE MONETIZATION GAP
With 72% of victims refusing to pay, threat actors faced a growing inventory of exfiltrated data with no traditional monetization path. This drove innovation in alternative revenue streams: structured data processing, segmented purchasing, analytical reports.

→ THE INFRASTRUCTURE RESPONSE
Platforms like Leak Bazaar emerged to address the monetization gap. Instead of binary extortion, stolen data becomes a refinable asset with extended operational life through structured resale.

The result: organizations can no longer eliminate risk by refusing to pay. The threat calculus fundamentally changed in 2025.

#Ransomware #ThreatIntelligence #CISO #RiskManagement #DataBreach
posts.cta: Reassess your incident response strategy to account for long-term data monetization risks

visual.headline: Ransomware 2025: The Economics of Ecosystem Evolution

posts.hook: The initial access broker market underwent fundamental transformation in 2025-2026. Price deflation, volume expansion, and industrial-scale automation redefined the economics
posts.body:
The initial access broker market underwent fundamental transformation in 2025-2026. Price deflation, volume expansion, and industrial-scale automation redefined the economics of criminal access.

→ PRICE COLLAPSE
DWIQ operational data shows average network access prices fell from $1,427 in Q1 2023 to $439 in Q1 2026 — a 69% deflation driven by automation, AI-assisted tooling, and infostealer log oversupply.

→ MARKET SEGMENTATION
While bulk access became commodity-priced, premium access types maintained high valuations: domain administrator credentials, Citrix environments with full internal reach, and cloud management consoles still command thousands to tens of thousands of dollars.

→ VOLUME EXPLOSION
DWIQ observed approximately 5,800 access offers in Q3 2025, up from roughly 2,700 in late 2024. The proliferation of automated tooling lowered technical barriers for individual operators to generate high-volume access offers.

→ INDUSTRIAL TOOLING
The $50,000 distributed brute-force system targeting 8 enterprise VPN protocols represents complete market maturation: enterprise pricing, lifetime support, custom development, professional documentation. Criminal tooling adopted legitimate software practices.

→ PROTOCOL TARGETING
Fortinet, SonicWall, and Cisco VPN products consistently dominated access-type distributions throughout 2025. The industrial tooling specifically targets these platforms alongside five additional protocols in unified attack pipelines.

This transformation enabled the ransomware volume increases observed in 2025 while simultaneously contributing to payment rate decline through access commoditization.

#ThreatIntelligence #AccessBrokers #VPNSecurity #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb
posts.cta: Audit your remote access infrastructure against industrial-scale automation threats

visual.headline: Initial Access Market Transformation: From Boutique to Industrial

posts.hook: Traditional post-breach monitoring assumes stolen data appears as raw dumps on leak sites. Leak Bazaar changes this assumption completely. Organizations must now monitor for
posts.body:
Traditional post-breach monitoring assumes stolen data appears as raw dumps on leak sites. Leak Bazaar changes this assumption completely. Organizations must now monitor for structured resale across extended timeframes.

→ PROCESSING TIMELINE EXTENSION
Leak Bazaar's four-stage pipeline — automated filtering, database reverse engineering, categorization, manual validation — means stolen data has extended operational life. Raw dumps become refined intelligence products over weeks or months.

→ SEGMENTED PURCHASING MODEL
Buyers can purchase specific data categories rather than entire dumps: quarterly financial reports, M&A data, R&D material, source code, personal data. This increases the likelihood of partial data monetization across multiple transactions.

→ SHARED VS. EXCLUSIVE OPTIONS
Shared purchases (lower price, data remains available) enable multiple resales of the same material. A single breach can generate revenue through repeated transactions with different buyer profiles over extended periods.

→ ANALYTICAL REPORT GENERATION
Platforms generate structured reports identifying OFAC violations, shadow accounting, unpublished results. These reports serve as negotiation pressure tools, extending the threat timeline beyond initial data theft.

→ MONITORING IMPLICATIONS
Post-breach monitoring must account for: refined data products, not just raw dumps; extended monetization timelines, not just immediate publication; segmented data sales across categories; analytical derivative products; multiple resale transactions.

Access to processing platforms becomes an intelligence priority for organizations seeking to understand their true post-breach risk exposure.

#ThreatIntelligence #DataBreach #IncidentResponse #CISO #SecurityOperations
posts.cta: Expand your post-breach monitoring to include structured data marketplaces and derivative products

visual.headline: Post-Breach Monitoring Evolution: Raw Dumps vs. Structured Products

posts.hook: Everyone's celebrating the 28% ransomware payment rate like it's a victory. It's not. It's the worst possible outcome for defenders. Lower payment rates don't reduce risk —
posts.body:
Everyone's celebrating the 28% ransomware payment rate like it's a victory. It's not. It's the worst possible outcome for defenders. Lower payment rates don't reduce risk — they multiply it.

When 72% of victims refuse to pay, threat actors don't pack up and go home. They innovate. They build platforms like Leak Bazaar to convert exfiltrated data into structured, sellable intelligence products. They develop industrial-scale automation tools for $50,000 that target eight VPN protocols simultaneously.

The result? More attacks (50% YoY increase), longer data monetization timelines, and sophisticated resale mechanisms that extend breach impact for months or years.

Refusing to pay used to end the threat. Now it just shifts the monetization strategy. Organizations thinking they've "won" by not paying are discovering their data has a much longer criminal shelf life than anticipated.

#Ransomware #ThreatIntelligence #CISO #RiskManagement
posts.cta: Challenge your assumptions about how payment decisions affect long-term risk exposure

Your VPN isn't protecting you anymore. It's a target-rich environment. When criminals can buy industrial-scale brute-force systems for $50,000 that automatically target Fortinet, SonicWall, Cisco, and five other enterprise protocols, the entire remote access paradigm breaks down.

We're still defending like it's 2019: deploy VPN, configure MFA, monitor logs. But threat actors moved to industrial automation with enterprise-grade tooling, professional support, and custom development options.

Masscan integration, honeypot filtering, proxy rotation, centralized management, fault tolerance. This isn't script kiddie tooling — it's productized software that treats your remote access infrastructure like a manufacturing target.

The fundamental assumption underlying remote access security — that manual attacks don't scale — is now obsolete.

#VPNSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec #SecurityOperations

posts.hook: Access prices fell 69% in three years. Security teams are treating this like good news. It's actually the most dangerous trend in cybersecurity. When criminal services become
posts.body:
Access prices fell 69% in three years. Security teams are treating this like good news. It's actually the most dangerous trend in cybersecurity. When criminal services become commodity-priced, the entire threat landscape shifts.

Network access dropped from $1,427 to $439 due to automation, AI tooling, and infostealer oversupply. But commoditization doesn't reduce demand — it explodes volume. DWIQ observed access offers jump from 2,700 to 5,800 in less than a year.

Cheap access enables more threat actors to enter the ecosystem. Lower barriers to entry mean more ransomware operators, more data brokers, more sophisticated monetization platforms.

The security industry optimized defenses for expensive, targeted attacks. We're getting cheap, automated, high-volume exploitation instead.

#ThreatIntelligence #AccessBrokers #CyberSecurity #RiskManagement
posts.cta: Reassess your threat model for high-volume, commodity-priced attack scenarios

visual.headline: The Access Commoditization Trap: Lower Prices, Higher Volume Risk

posts.hook: Industrial-scale brute-force systems targeting 8 VPN protocols simultaneously require industrial-scale countermeasures. Standard security controls weren't designed for this
posts.body:
Industrial-scale brute-force systems targeting 8 VPN protocols simultaneously require industrial-scale countermeasures. Standard security controls weren't designed for this threat category.

ACTION 1: AGGRESSIVE LOCKOUT POLICIES
Enforce account lockout after 3 failed attempts with extended lockout periods (minimum 30 minutes) on all internet-facing VPN and remote access endpoints. Prioritize Fortinet SSL-VPN, SonicWall SSL-VPN, Cisco AnyConnect, and RDWeb gateways — the primary targets in the $50,000 toolset.

ACTION 2: DISTRIBUTED ATTACK DETECTION
Monitor authentication logs for low-and-slow patterns: multiple failed attempts across different accounts from rotating source IPs. Traditional threshold-based alerting misses proxy-rotated automation. Implement behavioral analysis that detects coordinated credential testing across your entire remote access surface.

ACTION 3: COMPREHENSIVE MFA AUDIT
Audit all remote access infrastructure for accounts lacking MFA enforcement. Default accounts, test accounts, service accounts, and migrated accounts remain primary targets for automated brute-force campaigns. Zero exceptions.

ACTION 4: MANAGEMENT INTERFACE RESTRICTION
Restrict all management interfaces to trusted IP ranges. Reduce the externally reachable attack surface for scanning tools like Masscan. If management access requires internet exposure, implement additional authentication layers beyond standard user access controls.

Industrial threats require industrial responses. Standard security hygiene is insufficient when facing $50,000 automation platforms with enterprise support structures.

#VPNSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #InfoSec #SecurityOperations #IncidentResponse
posts.cta: Implement these countermeasures and test them against distributed, automated attack scenarios
